album.title: K-Pop Demon Hunters (Soundtrack from the Netflix Film)

wiki.summary: Fierce, unapologetic, and full of attitude, "How It’s Done" is an electrifying anthem performed by the fictional girl group HUNTR/X in the 2025 Sony Pictures Animation film K-Pop Demon Hunters. It released on August 27, 2025 as a promotional single for the soundtrack before being officially released as a single in January 13, 2026. In the song, members Rumi, Zoey, and Mira channel their confidence, strength, and unity as they take on both supernatural threats and the expectations of stardom. Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: Fierce, unapologetic, and full of attitude, "How It’s Done" is an electrifying anthem performed by the fictional girl group HUNTR/X in the 2025 Sony Pictures Animation film K-Pop Demon Hunters. It released on August 27, 2025 as a promotional single for the soundtrack before being officially released as a single in January 13, 2026. In the song, members Rumi, Zoey, and Mira channel their confidence, strength, and unity as they take on both supernatural threats and the expectations of stardom. Through bold lyrics and a hard-hitting beat, they assert their power as demon hunters and performers, refusing to be underestimated or held back. The recurring line "how it’s done" becomes a declaration of dominance, whether on stage or in battle, while also symbolising their mastery of both their craft and their identities. The song reflects the film’s central theme of fearlessly embracing who you are, even in the face of darkness. With lyrics full of sass, strength, and unity, the song celebrates girl power, teamwork, and fearlessness as they take down evil in style, showing the world how it’s done. The track is performed by the film’s singing voice cast: K-pop artist EJAE as Rumi, Korean-American singer REI AMI as Zoey, and American R&B singer Audrey Nuna as Mira. How It's Done peaked at #8 on the Billboard Hot 100 in September 2025, becoming the second top 10 for Huntr/x after their #1 hit "Golden" and alongside Saja Boy's "Your Idol" & "Soda Pop" brung a total of 5 weeks for the 4 soundtrack singles in the top 10 simultaneously. Read more on Last.fm. User-contributed text is available under the Creative Commons By-SA License; additional terms may apply.
